sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन । मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
hindiTranslation: कर्तव्यकर्म करनेमें ही तेरा अधिकार है, फलोंमें कभी नहीं ।  अतः तू कर्मफलका हेतु भी मत बन और तेरी अकर्मण्यतामें भी आसक्ति न हो ॥
englishTranslation: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits. Do not become the cause of the result, and do not cling to inaction.
essence: Action belongs to you; the result does not.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: You control your action, not what it produces. Do your duty without chasing the reward or getting attached to doing nothing.
simpleInsight: There is relief in giving your full effort without carrying the burden of the result.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna begins rebuilding his sense of duty. Here Krishna draws the first hard line: act because it is yours to do, not because you can own what comes after.
modernRelevance: You send the apology text after midnight, then keep rereading it for the perfect reply. This verse cuts that loop: speak, act, and stop trying to control the response.
translationLiteral: कर्मणि (in action) / एव (only) / अधिकारः (right) / ते (your) / मा (not) / फलेषु (in fruits) / कदाचन (at any time) / मा (not) / कर्मफलहेतुः (cause of the fruit of action) / भूः (become) / मा (not) / ते (your) / सङ्गः (attachment) / अस्तु (let there be) / अकर्मणि (in inaction)
